data.title: IMF Urges Japan to Continue Rate Hikes Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description:
On April 4, Jin10 reported that despite the Middle East conflict posing a significant new risk to Japan's economic outlook, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has urged the Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates. According to Jin10, the IMF's recommendation comes as the market widely anticipates a potential rate hike by the Bank of Japan as early as April. The conflict-driven rise in oil prices and the weakening yen have increased import costs, exacerbating inflationary pressures in Japan.

The IMF stated that although economic growth is expected to slow, partly due to the war in Iran, moderate wage growth will support consumer spending. The risks to Japan's economic outlook and inflation are broadly balanced, with inflation projected to return to the Bank of Japan's 2% target level by 2027. As core inflation gradually aligns with the central bank's target, the IMF suggests that Japan should continue gradual rate hikes in a flexible, well-communicated, and data-dependent manner, moving towards a neutral interest rate level.

Furthermore, the IMF emphasized the importance of maintaining a flexible exchange rate mechanism, as it serves as a reliable buffer against external shocks.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to Allocate $80 Billion for Small Business Financing in Underserved U.S. Communities
data.description: JPMorgan Chase has announced its American Dream initiative, which aims to provide $80 billion in financing to small businesses across the United States. According to NS3.AI, the program is designed to support underserved communities, with a particular focus on minority-owned and low-to-moderate income businesses. This initiative is part of the bank's broader effort to promote economic growth and inclusivity in areas that have historically faced financial challenges.

data.title: Nevada court extends injunction against Kalshi sports betting market
data.description: A Nevada judge ruled that Kalshi's sports betting prediction market products are "materially indistinguishable" from betting activities, extending the previously issued temporary injunction and granting a preliminary injunction filed by the state's betting regulator, prohibiting Kalshi from continuing to offer related contract services in the state until further proceedings. The injunction, initially issued on March 20, covers sports, entertainment, and election-related prediction markets. The regulator believes the products should be governed by state betting laws, while Kalshi argues that they fall under federally regulated derivatives markets. The question of regulatory jurisdiction remains unresolved. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Middle East Conflict Threatens Global Supply Chains Amid Oil Shortage
data.description:
The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has entered its second month, posing a significant risk of exacerbating the global oil shortage crisis. According to BlockBeats, the disruption has severely restricted the transportation of oil and natural gas through the Strait of Hormuz, reducing global supply by approximately one-fifth.

This interruption has not only driven up fuel prices but also strained the supply of petrochemical products essential for manufacturing everyday items such as shoes, clothing, and plastic bags. As the prices of materials like plastic, rubber, and polyester rise, the pressure is spreading across consumer markets.

Asia, home to over half of the world's manufacturing and heavily reliant on imported oil and commodities, is experiencing the most significant impact. Dan Martin, co-head of business intelligence at Deloitte Xianchi Advisory, noted that the effects will quickly extend to all goods, including beer, noodles, chips, toys, and cosmetics, due to the increasing difficulty in sourcing plastic bottle caps, transport pallets, snack packaging bags, and containers.

Martin further explained that adhesives used in footwear and furniture, industrial lubricants for machinery, paints, and solvents required for cleaning processes are also dependent on petroleum-derived products.

data.title: Bitcoin Transaction Demonstrated Without Internet at BOSS Summit
data.description:
On April 4, a groundbreaking demonstration took place at the BOSS Summit in Dharamsala, India, showcasing a Bitcoin transaction conducted entirely without internet access. According to BlockBeats, developer Bala executed the transaction using Mesh Radio, a wireless mesh network based on Meshtastic LoRa technology, bypassing traditional internet service providers, Wi-Fi, and cellular data.

The transaction was broadcast in real-time via radio waves directly into the Bitcoin mempool, completing the process from 'Off-Grid' to 'On-Chain.' This demonstration highlighted Bitcoin's resilience as an unseizable currency, particularly useful in scenarios involving censorship, internet outages, disasters, or uncertain times. Bitshala officials emphasized that this is what they mean by building an unseizable currency for uncertain times.

The relevant open-source repository has been made available, supporting the connection between Meshtastic devices and Bitcoin Core. It allows transaction data to be split into small packets, transmitted hop-by-hop across mesh nodes, and ultimately broadcast by an exit node to the global Bitcoin network.

The demonstration has sparked significant discussion within the community, being seen as a landmark advancement in the integration of mesh radio technology with Bitcoin. It proves that the Bitcoin network can maintain basic functionality even when traditional communication infrastructure fails.

data.title: Nevada Judge Extends Ban on Kalshi's Sports Contracts
data.description: A Nevada judge has extended a temporary ban on Kalshi's sports-related contracts, indicating plans to grant a preliminary injunction requested by the Nevada Gaming Control Board. According to NS3.AI, the judge prolonged the restraining order initially issued on March 20 by an additional two weeks to finalize the language of the injunction. The original order also prohibited entertainment and election-related bets.

data.title: Bitdeer Reports Zero Bitcoin Holdings After Recent Sales
data.description: Nasdaq-listed mining company Bitdeer has disclosed its latest Bitcoin holdings data. According to Odaily, as of the week ending April 3, Bitdeer produced 149.7 BTC through mining activities, all of which have been sold, resulting in the company currently holding zero Bitcoin.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Downs Enemy Drone Over Isfahan
data.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced on the 4th local time that it successfully shot down an enemy MQ-1 drone over Isfahan Province. According to Odaily, the IRGC's public relations department confirmed the incident, highlighting the ongoing tensions in the region. The MQ-1 drone, known for its reconnaissance capabilities, was intercepted as it entered Iranian airspace. The IRGC has not disclosed the origin of the drone or provided further details on the operation. This event underscores the heightened security measures and vigilance maintained by Iran's military forces.

data.title: Bitcoin's Price Range Sparks Analyst Debate on Future Momentum
data.description: Bitcoin continues to trade between $60,000 and $74,000 for the second consecutive month following its February low. According to NS3.AI, analysts are split on the asset's future trajectory. Michael van de Poppe suggests that a break above $71,000 could enhance momentum, while other experts caution that reaching the next all-time high might take more time.

data.title: Bitdeer maintained zero Bitcoin holdings, selling 149.7 BTC this week.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed mining company Bitdeer disclosed its latest Bitcoin holdings data. As of the week ending April 3, its mining output was 149.7 BTC, all of which have been sold, and it currently maintains a zero Bitcoin holding.

data.title: Israel Intensifies Military Actions Against Iran
data.description:
On April 4, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) will continue to collaborate with the United States in intensifying military operations against Iran. According to BlockBeats, Netanyahu stated during a situation assessment with senior Israeli officials that the IDF is actively targeting Iranian military commanders and bombing infrastructure such as bridges.

Netanyahu further revealed that the Israeli military has destroyed approximately 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. This action aims to weaken the financial resources of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and diminish its ability to produce weapons on a large scale.

data.title: Iran Conflict Enters Fifth Week, Market Impact Remains Uncertain
data.description: According to Jin10, a research report by China International Capital Corporation (CICC) highlights the ongoing complexity of the Iran conflict, now in its fifth week. The situation has evolved beyond initial market expectations, with a resolution still distant and the path to de-escalation fraught with potential setbacks. Investors are primarily concerned with whether the market has reached a bottom. To address this, it is crucial to assess whether the situation will escalate further. Even amid uncertainty, asset pricing can be evaluated for adequacy. Additionally, pricing differences across various assets and industries can lead to distinct value propositions. CICC identifies April as a critical period to determine if financial market disturbances will translate into tangible economic impacts. In terms of asset pricing, bonds, gold, and copper are viewed pessimistically, while equity markets generally underprice pessimistic scenarios. The report advises a strategic approach: investing in adequately priced assets, holding beneficial assets without chasing high prices, and using low-volatility dividends or reducing positions to hedge against market fluctuations.

data.title: AI TRENDS | ZetaChain Integrates Google's Gemma 4 AI Model into Anuma AI Layer
data.description: ZetaChain has integrated Google's newly launched Gemma 4 AI model into its Anuma AI layer, just a day after the model's official release. According to NS3.AI, ZetaChain announced that the Gemma 4 model is now accessible on Anuma without requiring separate installation. The company highlighted that Gemma 4 outperforms models that are 20 times its size.

data.title: Uniswap Introduces Automatic Routing Feature Across Its Platforms
data.description: Uniswap has launched an automatic routing feature on its web application, wallet, and API. According to Foresight News, this new functionality eliminates the need for a whitelist process, allowing users to build their own Hooks and deploy liquidity pools to start distribution from day one. As a security measure, each transaction is simulated, and if the chosen path might lead to a failure, the system automatically selects an alternative route for the transaction. Hooks that do not meet the automatic routing standards will still undergo manual review by the team.

data.title: Prediction Market Trading Volume Rises in March 2026
data.description: Prediction market trading volume reached $25.7 billion in March 2026, marking a 10.6% increase from February's $23.2 billion, according to NS3.AI. Kalshi led the market with a trading volume of $13.1 billion, while Polymarket followed closely with $10.6 billion.

data.title: Hermes Agent v0.7.0 Introduces Scalable Plugin System
data.description:
Nous Research has announced the release of Hermes Agent v0.7.0. According to Foresight News, the latest version features a scalable plugin system for memory, allowing users to switch between different backends or build their own. The built-in memory is immediately available and supports integration with six third-party providers, offering users the option to select 'hermes memory setup.'

Additional highlights include a credential pool for multiple providers, enabling automatic rotation and failover of multiple API keys per provider. The Camofox browser offers a stealth browsing backend for persistent sessions, VNC debugging, and automatic installation. Inline difference previews allow users to view changes in file writing and patch display tools before the agent proceeds.
